mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects rising aggregate wage income, which boosts consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations. This income channel transmits directly into equity demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS are followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An effective federal funds rate above 2.0% shifts the risk-free rate above the S&P 500 earnings yield, triggering portfolio reallocation from equities to cash and raising discount rates on future profits. This level effect generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of elevated consumer prices (CPIAUCSL above 280) and rising policy rates (FEDFUNDS) erodes real household disposable income and compresses corporate profit margins through higher input and debt-servicing costs. This interaction produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A regression interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day S&P 500 returns with a t-statistic magnitude below 2.0 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, stronger industrial production (INDPRO) signals overheating that invites additional Fed hikes rather than positive earnings surprises. The resulting higher terminal rate expectations drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns following INDPRO increases are not significantly negative during periods when FEDFUNDS is rising (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.2 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that precedes declines in personal income and consumption. This contractionary impulse generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) captures a broad demand slowdown across interest-rate-sensitive and consumer sectors. The interaction of these two demand proxies amplifies recession probability and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The product of normalized HOUST and RSAFS changes fails to exhibit a negative rank correlation with S&P 500 60-day returns (Rank IC > -0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving factory utilization and inventory restocking that supports near-term earnings surprises. This supply-side momentum generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 290 indicates entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain a restrictive policy stance, elevating the equity risk premium and discount rates. This inflation threshold generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 290.
